questions.q: For a commutative ring $R$, the underlying set of $\operatorname{Spec}(R)$ consists of:
questions.explain: By definition $\operatorname{Spec}(R)=\{\mathfrak p\subset R : \mathfrak p\text{ prime}\}$. Maximal ideals are closed points, but non-maximal primes (like $(0)$ in $\mathbb Z$) give generic points.

questions.q: How many points does $\operatorname{Spec}\mathbb{Z}/12\mathbb{Z}$ have? (Count prime ideals of $\mathbb Z/12\mathbb Z$.)
questions.explain: Prime ideals of $\mathbb Z/12$ correspond to primes $p$ with $p\mid 12$, namely $p=2$ and $p=3$. So there are $2$ points.

choices: $V(I)=\{\mathfrak p : I\subseteq \mathfrak p\}$ for ideals $I\subseteq R$
questions.explain: The vanishing loci $V(I)=\{\mathfrak p\supseteq I\}$ define the Zariski topology. They are closed under arbitrary intersection and finite union.

questions.q: On an affine scheme $\operatorname{Spec}(R)$, the stalk of the structure sheaf $\mathcal O$ at a prime $\mathfrak p$ is:
questions.explain: By construction the stalk $\mathcal O_{\mathfrak p}$ is the localization $R_{\mathfrak p}$, a local ring with maximal ideal $\mathfrak p R_{\mathfrak p}$ and residue field $\kappa(\mathfrak p)=\operatorname{Frac}(R/\mathfrak p)$.

questions.q: For the distinguished open $D(f)=\{\mathfrak p : f\notin \mathfrak p\}\subset \operatorname{Spec}(R)$, the ring of sections is:
questions.explain: $\mathcal O(D(f))=R_f$: inverting $f$ allows $1/f$ as a regular function exactly on the locus where $f$ does not vanish.

questions.q: The residue field $\kappa(\mathfrak p)$ at the generic point $\mathfrak p=(0)$ of $\operatorname{Spec}\mathbb{Z}$ is $\mathbb Q$. What is its characteristic?
questions.explain: $\mathbb Q$ has characteristic $0$. By contrast $\kappa((p))=\mathbb F_p$ at a closed point has characteristic $p$ — different points can have residue fields of different characteristics.

questions.q: The projective line $\mathbb P^1_k$ is built by gluing two copies of $\mathbb A^1_k=\operatorname{Spec}k[x]$ and $\operatorname{Spec}k[y]$ along the overlap $\{x\ne 0\}\cong\{y\ne 0\}$ via:
questions.explain: The transition is $y=1/x$: on the overlap $\operatorname{Spec}k[x,x^{-1}]=\operatorname{Spec}k[y,y^{-1}]$ with $y$ inverse to $x$. The two origins become $0$ and $\infty$.

questions.q: How many closed points does $\mathbb P^1_{\mathbb F_q}$ have over a finite field $\mathbb F_q$? Give the answer for $q=5$.
questions.explain: $|\mathbb P^1(\mathbb F_q)|=q+1$. For $q=5$ that is $5+1=6$ closed points: the $5$ values of $x\in\mathbb F_5$ plus the point at infinity.

questions.q: Which statement about $\mathbb P^1_k$ is TRUE?
choices: $\mathbb P^1_k$ is not affine but is covered by two affine opens
$\mathbb P^1_k$ has no affine open subsets
questions.explain: $\mathbb P^1_k$ is the classic non-affine scheme. Global sections of $\mathcal O$ are just $k$ (too few for an affine of positive dimension), yet it is covered by two affine charts $\mathbb A^1_k$.
